prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis has been performed for benzaldehyde at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set, tier 'standard', gas phase (no solvent). Charge = 0 and multiplicity = 1 were used, and the Mulliken partial charges scheme was applied. DFT calculations were carried out in PySCF. The most electrophilic atom was identified as carbon (index 3, atom label 3, f+ = 0.39938968). Warnings: 
(1) "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)", 
(2) "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)", 
(3) "common header present." The computation passed the integrity checks (status ok, trustworthy computation).
